steps.title: Recognize the implicit comparison
steps.content: London Array's output (630 MW) is presented first, then Hornsea Project One is introduced as generating the most power of any single offshore farm (1,218 MW). Placing two farms with different power outputs back-to-back signals you're meant to compare them.

steps.title: Select the transition expressing comparison
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast — London Array's 630 MW output is placed beside Hornsea Project One's 1,218 MW to show the scale difference. The blank needs a transition signaling that side-by-side comparison.
  • Choice A — "As a result,": signals cause-effect, so fails — Hornsea's output is not caused by London Array's output. ✗
  • Choice B — "That is,": signals restatement or clarification, so fails — Hornsea's figure is a new, different fact, not a rephrasing of London Array's. ✗
  • Choice C — "By comparison,": signals a direct contrast between two measured quantities, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "For instance,": signals a specific example of a general claim, so fails — no general claim precedes the Hornsea sentence. ✗
This matches choice C — C is correct.
